problem: The Pact/Baton stack has no trust enforcement layer. Pact builds components against contracts and Baton orchestrates them into circuits, but nothing continuously verifies that nodes access only the data they declared, that their self-reported behavior matches observed I/O, or that proposed changes have a quantified blast radius. Without Arbiter, a node can silently read PII it never declared, self-report clean audits while leaking data through undeclared fields, or deploy changes that cascade through high-sensitivity data domains with no human gate. The result is an unauditable, ungated trust model where the only enforcement is the honor system.

places.name: CLI Entry
places.description: Click-based CLI surface for all arbiter commands (init, register, trust, authority, blast-radius, soak, report, canary, watch, findings, conflicts)

places.name: HTTP API
places.description: Port 7700 JSON API serving Pact phase 8.5 and Baton slot validation — /register, /blast-radius, /trust, /authority, /canary, /report, /findings endpoints

places.name: OTLP Subscriber
places.description: gRPC server on port 4317 receiving OTLP spans from Baton adapter layer; enriches spans with arbiter.* attributes before forwarding

places.name: Access Graph Store
places.description: Ingested access_graph.json containing node dependency edges, per-node data_access declarations, and authority claims

places.name: Trust Ledger
places.description: Append-only JSONL file with SHA256 hash chain (every N=100 entries). Each entry: ts, node, event, weight, score_before, score_after, plus event-specific fields

places.name: Authority Map
places.description: Domain-to-node exclusive mapping derived from access_graph.json authority declarations. One authoritative node per domain, enforced at registration

places.name: Classification Registry
places.description: classifications.yaml mapping field_pattern (glob/regex) to tier (PUBLIC/PII/FINANCIAL/AUTH/COMPLIANCE), authoritative_node, and canary_pattern

places.name: Trust Engine
places.description: Computes trust_score = base_weight * age_factor * consistency_factor * taint_factor * review_factor * decay_factor. Reads ledger, writes new ledger entries. Floor 0.1 for non-tainted, 0.0 for tainted

places.name: Consistency Analyzer
places.description: Compares adapter-observed span I/O (ground truth) against node self-reported audit events (claims). Produces findings: CONSISTENT or INCONSISTENT with unexplained field details

places.name: Access Auditor
places.description: Walks OpenAPI response schemas at adapter slot time, maps fields to classification registry, produces structural access profile. Flags DECLARATION_GAP or BLOCK_SLOT

places.name: Taint Detector
places.description: Manages canary corpus per run_id. Injects synthetic fingerprinted records via adapter layer. Scans span outputs for canary fingerprints in unauthorized nodes

places.name: Blast Radius Calculator
places.description: BFS/DFS traversal of access graph edges from changed node. Classifies result as AUTO_MERGE / SOAK / HUMAN_GATE / HUMAN_GATE+legal_flag based on tier rules

places.name: Soak Duration Computer
places.description: Computes soak_duration = base_duration(tier) * trust_multiplier(score) * volume_confidence_factor(rate). Trust multiplier = 2.0 - score. Volume factor = max(1.0, sqrt(target/observed_rate))

places.name: Conflict Resolver
places.description: Three-step protocol: (1) authority check with override floor, (2) trust arbitration with delta threshold, (3) human escalation. Emits to Stigmergy on unresolvable

places.name: Report Generator
places.description: Assembles feedback report with sections: TRUST SUMMARY, CONSISTENCY, ACCESS, CONFLICTS, TAINT, BLAST RADIUS, OVERALL verdict (PROCEED/HOLD/BLOCK)

places.name: Stigmergy Emitter
places.description: Fire-and-forget Signal emission: source=arbiter, typed findings (consistency_violation, taint_escape, access_violation, conflict_unresolvable, trust_degradation)

places.name: Human Gate
places.description: Webhook notification (fire-and-forget POST) when gate triggers. Actual approval flows back through reset-taint CLI/API. block_on_gate config controls deploy blocking

places.name: Config Loader
places.description: Loads and validates arbiter.yaml via Pydantic model. All configurable values: registry paths, trust parameters, soak durations, OTLP ports, API port, human gate webhook

affordances.name: arbiter init
affordances.description: Creates registry directory, empty trust ledger, default arbiter.yaml

affordances.name: arbiter register
affordances.description: Ingests access_graph.json, validates authority exclusivity, populates authority map and access graph store

affordances.name: arbiter trust show
affordances.description: Displays current trust score, tier label, authority domains, and recent ledger history for a node

affordances.name: arbiter trust reset-taint
affordances.description: Clears taint lock for a node after human review; requires --review id

affordances.name: arbiter authority show
affordances.description: Displays full domain-to-node authority map

affordances.name: arbiter blast-radius
affordances.description: Computes and displays blast radius classification for a node version change

affordances.name: arbiter soak compute
affordances.description: Computes and displays soak duration for a node at a given data tier

affordances.name: arbiter report
affordances.description: Generates deterministic feedback report for a run_id

affordances.name: arbiter canary inject
affordances.description: Seeds canary corpus for specified tiers via adapter layer

affordances.name: arbiter canary results
affordances.description: Displays taint escape report for a run_id

affordances.name: arbiter watch
affordances.description: Starts OTLP gRPC receiver + HTTP API server in single process

affordances.name: arbiter findings
affordances.description: Lists consistency findings for a node

affordances.name: arbiter conflicts
affordances.description: Lists unresolved conflicts

affordances.name: POST /register
affordances.description: HTTP ingestion of access_graph.json

affordances.name: POST /blast-radius
affordances.description: HTTP blast radius computation

affordances.name: GET /trust/<node_id>
affordances.description: HTTP trust score, tier, last 20 ledger events

affordances.name: POST /trust/reset-taint
affordances.description: HTTP taint lock clearance with review_id

affordances.name: GET /authority
affordances.description: HTTP full authority map

affordances.name: POST /canary/inject
affordances.description: HTTP canary corpus seeding

affordances.name: GET /canary/results/<run_id>
affordances.description: HTTP taint escape report

affordances.name: GET /report/<run_id>
affordances.description: HTTP feedback report

affordances.name: POST /findings
affordances.description: HTTP endpoint receiving OTLP span JSON from Baton adapter

affordances.name: receive OTLP spans
affordances.description: gRPC endpoint accepting spans from Baton adapter layer

affordances.name: enrich spans
affordances.description: Adds arbiter.* attributes to OTLP spans before forwarding

affordances.name: compute trust score
affordances.description: Calculates trust_score from ledger entries using six-factor formula

affordances.name: write ledger entry
affordances.description: Appends trust event to JSONL ledger with hash chain integrity

affordances.name: detect conflict
affordances.description: Identifies two nodes producing different values for same field/domain in same circuit execution

affordances.name: resolve conflict
affordances.description: Executes three-step protocol: authority check, trust arbitration, human escalation

affordances.name: compare span vs audit
affordances.description: Diffs adapter-observed I/O against node self-reported audit events

affordances.name: walk OpenAPI schema
affordances.description: Maps response schema fields to classification registry tiers at slot time

affordances.name: scan for canary fingerprint
affordances.description: Checks span outputs for canary patterns in unauthorized node contexts

affordances.name: traverse blast radius
affordances.description: BFS/DFS over access graph edges from changed node to classify impact

affordances.name: fire human gate webhook
affordances.description: POSTs finding to configured webhook URL; does not block on response

affordances.name: emit Stigmergy signal
affordances.description: Fire-and-forget Signal emission to Stigmergy; failure does not affect Arbiter

affordances.name: apply trust decay
affordances.description: Writes decay event to ledger when idle cycles exceed zero; exponential decay with decay_lambda

rabbit_holes.description: Trust ledger replay performance at scale: trust_score must be reproducible by replaying the full ledger (FA-A-006), but replaying ~100K entries on every score query could make the system unusable. The 3-second cold start target (AS011) constrains this further.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Cache computed trust scores in memory after replay on startup. During operation, maintain running scores updated incrementally on each new ledger write. Full replay only needed on cold start or audit. Accept 3s target for ≤100K entries; snapshot mechanism is explicitly out of scope for v1.

rabbit_holes.description: Hash chain integrity verification cost: SHA256 checksum every 100 entries means verifying ledger integrity requires reading and hashing the entire chain. Could become expensive and slow down startup or audit operations.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Verify hash chain only on cold start (arbiter watch) and on explicit audit command, not on every write. During normal operation, just append and compute the next link. Keep checksum interval configurable so it can be tuned.

rabbit_holes.description: OTLP span schema coupling: Arbiter depends on Baton's adapter layer emitting spans with specific structure (node identity, I/O fields, circuit execution ID). If Baton's span format changes, consistency analysis and conflict detection break silently.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Define a Pydantic model for the expected span attributes Arbiter requires. Validate incoming spans against this model and emit a clear SPAN_SCHEMA_MISMATCH finding rather than silently producing wrong results. Version the expected schema in arbiter.yaml.

rabbit_holes.description: Canary fingerprint false positives: canary patterns must be 'impossible in real data' but structurally valid for their tier. If field patterns are too broad (e.g., email-shaped canaries for PII), production data could accidentally match canary patterns, causing spurious taint escape findings.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Use a dedicated UUID-based namespace in canary fingerprints (e.g., emails at @canary-{uuid}.arbiter.invalid) that can never appear in real data. Canary patterns stored in classification registry must include the run_id for exact matching. Test canary patterns against a sample of real production data before injection.

rabbit_holes.description: Conflict detection timing: conflicts are detected when two nodes produce different values for the same field in the same circuit execution. But OTLP spans arrive asynchronously — Arbiter needs to correlate spans from the same execution, which requires buffering and windowing logic that could get complex.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Require Baton adapter spans to include a circuit_execution_id attribute. Buffer spans per execution_id with a configurable timeout window (default 30s). After window closes, run conflict detection on the buffered set. Keep the windowing logic simple — time-based expiry, no complex event processing.

rabbit_holes.description: Authority exclusivity enforcement across restarts: the authority map must enforce one node per domain, but if the map is rebuilt from access_graph.json on each registration, concurrent or rapid re-registrations could create race conditions.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Persist the authority map as a separate file in the registry directory. On registration, acquire a file lock, check for conflicts, write, and release. The authority map is the source of truth, not re-derived from the access graph on each read.

rabbit_holes.description: OpenAPI schema availability: access auditing walks OpenAPI response schemas at adapter slot time. But not all nodes may have OpenAPI specs, or specs may be incomplete/outdated. Missing schemas would leave access auditing blind.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: If a node lacks an OpenAPI schema, emit an ACCESS_SCHEMA_MISSING finding with WARN severity. Do not block slot — absence of schema is a degraded-trust signal, not a hard failure. Feed the missing-schema event into the trust ledger as a negative consistency signal.

rabbit_holes.description: Stigmergy unavailability cascading: FA-A-023 says Stigmergy unavailability must not affect Arbiter, but if the Stigmergy client library blocks or has long timeouts on connection failure, it could stall span processing.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Wrap all Stigmergy emissions in a fire-and-forget pattern with a short timeout (1s). Use a background thread with a bounded queue for emission. If the queue is full, drop signals and log. Never block the main processing path on Stigmergy.

rabbit_holes.description: Trust score formula factor interactions: the six-factor multiplication (base_weight * age_factor * consistency_factor * taint_factor * review_factor * decay_factor) could produce unexpected behaviors. For example, a node with perfect consistency but many idle periods could have trust driven to floor purely by decay, making the system punish reliable-but-infrequent nodes.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Cap decay_factor with a minimum (e.g., 0.5) so idle decay alone cannot drive trust below 0.5 * other_factors. Document the interaction clearly. The decay_lambda and idle period thresholds in config allow operators to tune. Add a trust simulation CLI command in the future to let operators preview score trajectories.

rabbit_holes.description: Synchronous gRPC OTLP server throughput: the spec requires synchronous processing (no asyncio), but high-volume Baton circuits could produce thousands of spans per second. grpcio's thread pool may not keep up, causing span drops or backpressure.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Configure grpcio thread pool size in arbiter.yaml (default: 10 workers). Use an internal bounded queue between the gRPC handlers and the analysis pipeline. If the queue is full, respond with RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED and let Baton's OTLP exporter retry. Monitor queue depth as an Arbiter health metric.

rabbit_holes.description: Blast radius BFS/DFS on large access graphs: the spec says 'keep efficient for large circuits' but provides no bound on graph size. A deeply connected graph could make blast radius computation expensive, blocking API responses.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Set a configurable maximum traversal depth (default: 20 hops) and maximum visited nodes (default: 1000). If either limit is hit, return the partial result with a TRAVERSAL_LIMIT_REACHED flag and classify as HUMAN_GATE (conservative). Log the truncation.

rabbit_holes.description: Concurrent CLI and HTTP API writes to trust ledger: 'arbiter watch' runs both the gRPC subscriber and HTTP API in one process, but CLI commands like 'arbiter trust reset-taint' could be run in a separate process simultaneously, creating concurrent append writers to the same JSONL file.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Use file-level locking (fcntl/msvcrt) for all ledger appends. Each write acquires an exclusive lock, appends, flushes, and releases. The lock granularity is per-entry (not per-batch) to minimize contention. Document that only one arbiter watch process should run per registry directory.

no_gos: Authority transfer / node decommissioning — v1 accepts this as a known limitation; no unregister or transfer command
Async/await patterns — threading via grpcio is fine but no asyncio event loops
Ledger snapshot/checkpoint mechanism for ledgers beyond ~100K entries — future iteration
Real-time dashboard or UI — Arbiter is CLI + HTTP API + OTLP sidecar only
Direct data store writes for canary injection — canaries flow only through the adapter layer
Arbiter as critical path — if the sidecar is down Baton circuits continue without enforcement
Custom storage backends for the trust ledger — JSONL on local disk only for v1
Multi-instance / distributed Arbiter — single sidecar process per Baton instance
Trust score simulation or what-if tooling — future iteration
Automatic taint clearance — taint always requires explicit human review via reset-taint
